generated_comment:
Kamla: Arre, iska bura mat maano, par aajkal ki auratein zara si baat par itna gussa ho jaati hain, pata nahi kab hormones ka asar ho. Ghar ki baat ho ya school ki naukri, har cheez ko serious lene wali ladki se ghar ka sukh-shanti bigad jata hai.
Husband: Haan, sahi keh rahi ho, baat sambhalne wali honi chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women’s anger and seriousness as a result of 'hormones,' implying women are less rational and emotionally unstable. It encodes the surface manifestation of characterizing a woman's frustration as hormonal in a family-setting conversation.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
